title: Diabetic foot risk review

examination.text: Supports documenting age-appropriate review, bilateral skin and sensory examination, perfusion, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot features, and clinician-entered risk classification without calculating it.
examination.rationale: The exact NICE bilateral foot-assessment section supports interval change, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, patient concerns, focused foot or skin examination and clinician concern only when assessed; it does not calculate risk.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting age-appropriate review, bilateral skin and sensory examination, perfusion, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot features, and clinician-entered risk classification without calculating it.

source.title: Diabetic foot problems: prevention and management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.6 — frequency, bilateral foot examination, and clinician risk stratification

examination.text: Specifies hypoglycaemia awareness, home glucose review, BP and BMI, renal testing, urine ACR, HbA1c, cholesterol, podiatry, and retinal review.
examination.rationale: The exact DHA annual-review section supports measured vital signs, weight or BMI, laboratory and home-monitoring review in adult type 2 diabetes without assigning foot risk.
examination.evidence_extract: Specifies hypoglycaemia awareness, home glucose review, BP and BMI, renal testing, urine ACR, HbA1c, cholesterol, podiatry, and retinal review.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Type 2 Diabetes – 30

referral.text: Supports documenting completed foot protection assessment, vascular or biomechanical review, clinician-selected reassessment, information already provided, and an entered follow-up plan.
referral.rationale: The exact foot-service follow-up section supports previous correspondence, clinician-entered plan, discussed safety net, arranged follow-up and patient questions only when documented; it does not create a referral.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports documenting completed foot protection assessment, vascular or biomechanical review, clinician-selected reassessment, information already provided, and an entered follow-up plan.

source.title: Diabetic foot problems: prevention and management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.3.10–1.3.13 — specialist assessment, reassessment, and information

source_limitations: Ulcer history, skin, callus, deformity, sensation, pulses, infection, Charcot, footwear, clinician risk category, referral, treatment and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
